name: ng-open-in-chat
title: Open in Chat (Angular)
description: Dropdown that opens the current page in an external chat (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, T3 Chat, ...). Provides preset providers and accepts custom entries. Built on @spartan-ng/brain popover. Mirrors React OpenInChat from @gremorie/rx-ai.

files.content: import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, computed, forwardRef, inject, InjectionToken, input, ViewEncapsulation, } from '@angular/core'; import { BrnPopover, BrnPopoverTrigger } from '@spartan-ng/brain/popover'; import { cn } from '@gremorie/ng-core'; /** * OpenIn — a "open this prompt in another chat app" menu. Mirrors React * `OpenIn` from `@gremorie/rx-ai`. * * React parity: * - `OpenIn` provides the `query` via context (here `OPEN_IN`); each provider * item builds its deep-link URL from that query and renders an external link * with the provider icon + title. * - Provider items: `OpenInChatGPT`, `OpenInClaude`, `OpenInT3`, `OpenInScira`, * `OpenInv0`, `OpenInCursor` — identical URL builders to React. * - Container parts: `OpenInTrigger` (default "Open in chat" button), * `OpenInContent` (w-[240px] menu), `OpenInItem`, `OpenInLabel`, * `OpenInSeparator`. * - `data-slot` names match what React actually renders: React composes * `DropdownMenu`, so the trigger/content/item/label/separator surfaces carry * the `dropdown-menu-*` slots. The `open-in` root slot and the per-provider * host slots (`open-in-chatgpt`, …) are NG structural adaptations — React's * Radix Root renders no DOM node and its provider items render the `` * directly via `asChild` — kept so each Angular host element stays * addressable. * * Divergence: React composes Radix `DropdownMenu`. `@spartan-ng/brain` ships * no menu primitive, so the Angular edition builds the menu on `BrnPopover` * (CDK overlay). Brain's popover content must live in a * `` referenced by the trigger via * `brnPopoverTriggerFor` — the consumer wires those two; everything inside the * content template stays the same compound anatomy. */ type ProviderKey = 'scira' | 'chatgpt' | 'claude' | 't3' | 'v0' | 'cursor'; const PROVIDERS: Record< ProviderKey, { title: string; createUrl: (q: string) => string } > = { scira: { title: 'Open in Scira', createUrl: (q) => `https://scira.ai/?${new URLSearchParams({ q })}`, }, chatgpt: { title: 'Open in ChatGPT', createUrl: (prompt) => `https://chatgpt.com/?${new URLSearchParams({ hints: 'search', prompt })}`, }, claude: { title: 'Open in Claude', createUrl: (q) => `https://claude.ai/new?${new URLSearchParams({ q })}`, }, t3: { title: 'Open in T3 Chat', createUrl: (q) => `https://t3.chat/new?${new URLSearchParams({ q })}`, }, v0: { title: 'Open in v0', createUrl: (q) => `https://v0.app?${new URLSearchParams({ q })}`, }, cursor: { title: 'Open in Cursor', createUrl: (text) => { const url = new URL('https://cursor.com/link/prompt'); url.searchParams.set('text', text); return url.toString(); }, }, }; export interface OpenInState { query: () => string; } export const OPEN_IN = new InjectionToken('OpenInState'); @Component({ selector: 'open-in', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [BrnPopover], encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ``, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in', class: 'contents' }, providers: [{ provide: OPEN_IN, useExisting: forwardRef(() => OpenIn) }], }) export class OpenIn implements OpenInState { /** The prompt text to forward to the chosen chat app. Mirrors React `query`. */ readonly query = input.required(); } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-trigger', standalone: true, imports: [BrnPopoverTrigger], encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` `, host: { 'data-slot': 'dropdown-menu-trigger', class: 'inline-flex' }, }) export class OpenInTrigger { /** Template ref of the `` to open. */ // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any readonly content = input.required(); readonly class = input(); protected readonly buttonClass = computed(() => cn( 'inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-1.5 whitespace-nowrap rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-4 text-sm font-medium transition-colors hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring', this.class(), ), ); } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-content', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ``, host: { 'data-slot': 'dropdown-menu-content', '[class]': 'hostClass()', }, }) export class OpenInContent { readonly class = input(); protected readonly hostClass = computed(() => cn( 'block w-[240px] overflow-hidden rounded-md border bg-popover p-1 text-popover-foreground shadow-md', this.class(), ), ); } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-item', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ``, host: { 'data-slot': 'dropdown-menu-item', '[class]': 'hostClass()', }, }) export class OpenInItem { readonly class = input(); protected readonly hostClass = computed(() => cn( 'relative flex cursor-default select-none items-center gap-2 rounded-sm px-2 py-1.5 text-sm outline-none transition-colors hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground', this.class(), ), ); } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-label', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ``, host: { 'data-slot': 'dropdown-menu-label', '[class]': 'hostClass()', }, }) export class OpenInLabel { readonly class = input(); protected readonly hostClass = computed(() => cn('block px-2 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold', this.class()), ); } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-separator', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ``, host: { 'data-slot': 'dropdown-menu-separator', role: 'separator', '[class]': 'hostClass()', }, }) export class OpenInSeparator { readonly class = input(); protected readonly hostClass = computed(() => cn('-mx-1 my-1 block h-px bg-muted', this.class()), ); } /** Shared base for the provider deep-link items. */ abstract class OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly ctx = inject(OPEN_IN); protected abstract readonly provider: ProviderKey; protected readonly title = () => PROVIDERS[this.provider].title; protected readonly href = computed(() => PROVIDERS[this.provider].createUrl(this.ctx.query()), ); protected readonly itemClass = 'relative flex cursor-default select-none items-center gap-2 rounded-sm px-2 py-1.5 text-sm outline-none transition-colors hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-chatgpt', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-chatgpt', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInChatGPT extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 'chatgpt'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-claude', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-claude', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInClaude extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 'claude'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-t3', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-t3', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInT3 extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 't3'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-scira', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-scira', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInScira extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 'scira'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-v0', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-v0', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInv0 extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 'v0'; } @Component({ selector: 'open-in-cursor', standalone: true, encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.None, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, template: ` {{ title() }} `, host: { 'data-slot': 'open-in-cursor', class: 'block' }, }) export class OpenInCursor extends OpenInProviderItem { protected readonly provider: ProviderKey = 'cursor'; }

export {
  OpenIn,
  OpenInTrigger,
  OpenInContent,
  OpenInItem,
  OpenInLabel,
  OpenInSeparator,
  OpenInChatGPT,
  OpenInClaude,
  OpenInT3,
  OpenInScira,
  OpenInv0,
  OpenInCursor,
  OPEN_IN,
} from './open-in-chat';
export type { OpenInState } from './open-in-chat';

usage.whenToUse: Any AI chat surface - prompt input, attachments display, and the composable parts that build an AI conversation UI.
usage.whenNotToUse: Plain form inputs unrelated to a conversational AI flow - use ng-core Button + a native textarea instead. Single-file image preview without metadata - use a plain tag plus ng-core.
bestPractices: Always nest a PromptInputTextarea inside PromptInput - the parent owns the state machine.
Wire PromptInputSubmit as a child element; it picks up state automatically (idle / streaming / error).
Use PromptInputToolbar for sticky bottom controls (model select, tools menu, submit).
Compose AttachmentList + AttachmentItem + slot primitives rather than reaching for a god-component.
Pass AttachmentData objects produced by toAttachmentData() rather than raw Files - it centralises media-type detection.
antipatterns: Listening to submit on the textarea directly - listen to the parent gremoriePromptSubmit output to receive the full payload.
Re-implementing the state machine in the host component - drive state by setting state as input or letting the parent compute it.
Mixing PromptInputSubmit with a custom submit button - the primitive already handles the keyboard contract (Cmd/Ctrl + Enter).
Rendering attachments without an AttachmentRemove slot when the surface is editable - users expect a way to delete.
Mixing attachment variants within the same list - inconsistency between items breaks scannability.

inputs.name: variant
inputs.description: AttachmentList variant - layout for the attachment row/grid.

outputs.name: submitted
outputs.description: Fired when the user submits via Cmd/Ctrl+Enter or by clicking PromptInputSubmit.

outputs.name: attachmentError
outputs.description: Fired when an attachment is rejected (bad MIME, too large, etc.).
